marketState: neutral-to-bullish: hot 8-16D weather demand vs bearish storage surplus and mixed technicals
summary: Front-month NG trades at $2.884, essentially fair value ($2.923 fair, band $2.423–$3.423) yet well below its seasonal median for this time of year (-8.4pp, -15.4% YTD vs -6.9% median). The dominant medium-term catalyst is weather: 8-16 day demand is running +40.6% above the 5-year average and above the top of its historical range, versus only +7.1% (mid-range) for 1-7 days out, and the fair-value curve itself rises from $2.923 now to $3.030 by +21D, echoing that bullish demand signal. Working against that, storage sits +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and managed money is net short 105.7k contracts at just the 8th percentile of 5-year positioning — bearish positioning that also raises short-squeeze risk if the heat materializes. Seasonality odds are muted and flagged low-reliability (only 38% chance of higher prices at 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%), and technicals are split, with a bullish EMA cross intraday (5m/15m) against a bearish daily/weekly trend (RSI ~37, death cross). Net read: a weather-driven bullish lean for the medium term, tempered by an oversupplied storage backdrop, with the near-term picture too mixed for a confident directional call.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D weather demand is +40.6% vs 5y and above range max, and the fair-value curve rises from $2.923 to $3.030 over 21 days while price stays FAIR — both argue for higher prices into peak cooling demand. Extreme managed-money short positioning (8th percentile, ratio 0.68) adds squeeze potential, partly offsetting the bearish +180 Bcf storage surplus and a low-reliability seasonal read (only 38% up-probability at 3-4 weeks).
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D demand forecasts fading back toward/below the 5y normal, or storage builds accelerating further above the current +180 Bcf surplus, would remove the fundamental case for higher prices.

shortTerm.rationale: Price sits almost exactly at fair value (front spread -$0.039, status FAIR) with no clear near-term edge, and technicals conflict sharply across timeframes — bullish EMA cross on 5m/15m versus bearish trend, death cross and RSI ~37 on 1d/1w. The futuresCurve dataset is null in this snapshot, removing a key short-term term-structure input.
shortTerm.invalidation: A decisive close above $2.95–3.00 or below $2.75 on volume would invalidate the wait in the respective direction.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for daily RSI/MACD to turn bullish (RSI>50, histogram positive) and price to reclaim $2.95–3.00, or a clean pullback holding $2.75–2.80 support, before taking short-term exposure; also watch for futuresCurve data to reappear for term-structure confirmation.
